title: Weekly incident email using a saved search
problem: Support leads need a weekly summary of open incidents matching specific criteria.
approach: Create a saved search, bind it to a quick action or scheduled process, and send the output using an email template.
steps: Create a filtered saved search for the required incident set.
Build the email template with the right output fields.
Configure a quick action or recurring process to run weekly.
Validate recipients, schedule, and output formatting.
outcome: The team receives a repeatable, structured operational report without manual preparation.

title: Auto-activate a ticket on customer response
problem: Tickets remain in waiting states even after the customer replies, slowing resolution.
approach: Use response triggers, workflow logic, or automation to change status and notify the assigned team.
steps: Identify the waiting states and response channels.
Configure trigger logic to detect customer response.
Update status and send internal notifications.
Test edge cases and duplicate updates.
outcome: Agents see reactivated work immediately and customer follow-up moves faster.

title: Handle non-compliant devices through policy action
problem: Devices that fall out of compliance must be remediated or restricted quickly.
approach: Use compliance rules, remediation steps, notifications, and access controls based on policy violations.
steps: Define the compliance rule and thresholds.
Configure remediation or notification action.
Test behavior on compliant and non-compliant devices.
Monitor exceptions and operational reporting.
outcome: Security and mobility teams can act consistently on policy violations.

title: Phased patch deployment
problem: A full patch rollout creates risk without staged validation.
approach: Create a ring-based deployment with pilot groups, production waves, monitoring, and exception handling.
steps: Create pilot and production device groups.
Deploy and monitor the pilot wave.
Review failures, exceptions, and readiness.
Continue staged rollout with reporting.
outcome: Patching is safer, more predictable, and easier to troubleshoot.

title: Simplify a warehouse picking workflow
problem: A warehouse picking flow contains too many steps and slows frontline users.
approach: Reduce screens, optimize prompts, support scanning, and reshape the browser-based task flow.
steps: Map the current operator journey.
Identify unnecessary interaction points.
Design a streamlined sequence for handheld execution.
Validate speed and error reduction with users.
outcome: Operators complete tasks faster with fewer interruptions.

title: Integrate Ivanti with an external approval service
problem: Approvals must be synchronized between Ivanti and another enterprise platform.
approach: Design a secure REST flow with payload mapping, status synchronization, and retry logic.
steps: Define source and target payloads.
Implement authentication and connectivity handling.
Map statuses and response outcomes.
Add logging, retries, and exception reporting.
outcome: Approvals remain synchronized and traceable across systems.
